narrative: Jindal Stainless and SAIL were the strongest health-safety performers depending on the lens. SAIL led employee and worker LTIFR, while Jindal led employee and worker TRIFR and had the lowest worker fatalities. JSW was generally mid-pack, particularly on LTIFR and fatalities. Tata was the laggard on employee LTIFR, employee TRIFR, worker TRIFR and affected employee/worker counts, though Tata was not the worst on worker fatalities and led rehabilitation support. Tata FY2022-23 is consolidated, so it includes Indian and international operations and is not fully like-for-like against the standalone peer disclosures. This matters because consolidated exposure adds variation in work practices, regulatory environments, contractor management systems and operational mix. Still, the size of Tata’s TRIFR and affected-person gap suggests a real safety performance challenge, not just boundary noise. In heavy BF-BOF steelmaking, the key exposure areas remain contractor maintenance, logistics, mobile equipment, height work, stored energy and hot-metal/process safety.
